beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check houseplants, morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Raj is an early riser; 6:30am fits his natural rhythm. Full energy to start the week.

beats.activity: Commute to First Hill office via bus.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure 9am–3pm between Westlake–U District is not on his route; bus is reliable. Early arrival lets him settle in before the work week.

beats.activity: Prep desk, review tax season files, early work.; Continue work, handle client calls and filings.
beats.reasoning: June 1 is early in summer but tax season wraps up mid-month; Raj uses quiet morning hours for focused prep.

beats.activity: Head to nearby bar downtown to catch USA vs Wales match.
beats.reasoning: World Cup match at 11am is a rare event Raj won't miss; Pioneer Square bars open early for sports. Short walk from First Hill.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales match, eat bar snacks and drink.
beats.reasoning: Raj watches the live 11am kickoff; adventurous eater enjoys bar fare. Match runs ~2 hours including halftime.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby Vietnamese restaurant.
beats.reasoning: Adventurous eater; Vietnamese cuisine fits his profile. Stays in Pioneer Square to finish the day nearby.

beats.activity: Commute back to First Hill office.
beats.reasoning: Return to finish afternoon work. Light Rail closure ends at 3pm but bus is reliable.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: client meetings, tax filings, end-of-day wrap-up.
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon session to round out the work week. Tax season requires focus; no early exit today.
Light Rail closure now lifted; bus remains the reliable option. Heading home with perfect sunny weather.

beats.activity: Tend to houseplants, prep sourdough dough for overnight rise, light dinner.; Film photography review, organize photos from recent hikes, wind down with a book.
beats.reasoning: Raj unwinds with his hobbies: plant care and sourdough baking. Early evening routine fits his rhythm; sourdough overnight ferment is planned.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather and couldn't resist the pull of the World Cup. Coffee hit different this morning knowing the match was coming—there's something about early June that makes you feel like anything's possible. The bus ride to First Hill was smooth enough, and I powered through the morning files without too much friction. But by 11am, I was done pretending I could focus. Slipped out to The Crocodile Bar in Pioneer Square and watched the USA take on Wales with a crowd of strangers who suddenly felt like friends. The energy was electric. Grabbed a bowl of phở afterward at Phở Hòa—nothing fancy, just what I needed. The afternoon at the office felt like going through motions after that rush. Got home and lost myself in the darkroom work, organizing prints from last month's hikes up near the Cascades. There's something grounding about reviewing those images. Started the sourdough for tomorrow, fingers crossed it rises right overnight.
thought_process: The weather report and World Cup match timing hit me differently than a normal Monday—it felt like permission to break routine, and I took it. Tax season's been grinding me down, so stepping out mid-day wasn't really a choice, more like a reflex. The transit closure didn't affect me since I'm bus-dependent anyway, but it made downtown feel less predictable somehow. I genuinely debated staying at the office all day, being responsible, but I'm 49 and learned long ago that those moments of spontaneity matter more than another two hours of filings. The phở grounded me back into work mode enough to finish the day professionally. Tonight, retreating into the darkroom and sourdough felt like the real reward—tangible, creative, mine. The hiking photos reminded me why I tolerate the spreadsheets.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
